content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 1, 1989

James J. Gallagher
Sonoma County Assessor
585 Fiscal Drive, Room 104F
Santa Rosa, CA 95403-2872

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-270

Dear Mr. Gallagher:

This is in response to your request for advice regarding your
responsibilities under the mass mailing provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

Does inclusion of your name and title, and of the name of
your office in a mass mailing violate the mass mailing prohib-
itions of the Act?

CONCLUSION

Inclusion of your name and title and of the name of your of-
fice in a mass mailing violates the mass mailing prohibitions of
the Act.

FACTS

When you assumed office in January, 1987, you initiated a
public information program in the Sonoma County Assessor's Office.
One element of the program consists of a card you have submitted
for our review. The card describes property tax exemptions avail-
able to homeowners and it is mailed out through the office of the
county recorder with each recorded deed that is returned to a new
property owner. The card also includes your name and title on one

¹ Government Code Section 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-89-270
Page 2

side and the name and telephone number of your office on the other
side. Approximately 28,000 deeds were recorded in your county
during 1988. You believe this mailing is essential to inform
property owners of tax relief benefits administered by the
assessor's office.

ANALYSIS

Section 89001 prohibits mass mailings at public expense. A
mass mailing consists of 200 or more substantially similar pieces
of mail sent in any one calendar month. (Section 82041.5, Regulation 18901(d), copy enclosed.) The card you have submitted for
our review constitutes a mass mailing because you mail it to new
homeowners in quantities greater than 200 pieces per calendar
month.

Regulation 18901, which implements the provisions of Section
89001, clarifies the prohibition as follows:

(a) Except as provided herein, a newsletter
or other mass mailing is "sent at public expense"
within the meaning of Government Code Section 89001
if any of the costs of design, production, printing
or distribution, is paid with public moneys...

Moreover, a newsletter or other mass mailing may not be sent
if: (1) The name of an elected officer or his or her photograph
appears on the document and the elected officer exercises direction or control over the content, production, or distribution of
the document, or (2) The document is sent at the request or
suggestion of the elected officer or his or her agent, or (3) The
document is signed by, or is designated as being from, the elected
officer or his or her office. (Regulation 18901(c)(1).)

Inclusion of your name and title and reference to your office
is also prohibited under Section (c)(2) of Regulation 18901, which
prohibits mass mailings if the name, office or other reference to
the elected officer appears in the document and the document is
prepared or sent in cooperation with the elected officer. Because
the card is prepared by your office and mailed at your request,
distribution of the card violates the mass mailing prohibition of
the Act.

However, mass mailings are not prohibited when they consist
of legal notices and other mailings sent as required by law and
any use of the elected officer's name, office, title or signature
is required in the notice or other mailing. (Regulation
18901(f)(6).)

Based on the information you have provided, it appears that
the mailing of the card you have submitted is not required by law.
Therefore, in order to bring the card into compliance with the
prohibitions of Section 89001 and Regulation 18901, you must

Our File No. A-89-270
Page 3

delete your name, title and reference to your office from the
card.

     I trust we have adequately addressed your inquiry.  Should
you have any further questions regarding this matter, do not
hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature: Blanca Breeze]

                                        By:  Blanca M. Breeze
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

KED/BMB:aa

Enclosure

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 27, 1989

James J. Gallagher
Sonoma County Assessor
585 Fiscal Drive, Room 104F
Santa Rosa, CA  95403-2872

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-89-270

Dear Mr. Gallagher:

     We are in receipt of your letter of June 28, 1989, in which
you request confirmation of telephone advice provided to you on
June 16, 1989.  Because your inquiry is a follow-up to your previ-
ous request for written advice, we incorporate by reference our
letter to you, No. A-89-270, dated June 1, 1989 (copy enclosed).

     This confirms that a mass mailing is not prohibited if it
meets all of the following criteria:

     (1)  The stationery, forms and envelopes used for the mailing
are the standard stationery, forms and envelopes of your agency;

     (2)  Your name appears only in the standard letterhead or
logotype of the stationery, forms or envelopes of your agency;

     (3)  The document does not include your photograph; and

     (4)  The document does not display your signature.
(Regulation 18901(c) and (e), copy enclosed.)

     You may, therefore, print the information contained in the
card you have submitted for our review (copy enclosed) on your
agency's stationery as long as the material makes no reference to
your name or office other than in the standard letterhead of your
agency.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

File No. A-89-270
Page 2


Should you have any further questions regarding this matter,
do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure

James J. Gallagher
Sonoma County
Assessor

585 Fiscal Drive
Room 104F
Santa Rosa, CA 95403-2872
(707) 527-2541

June 28, 1989

Ms. Kathryn E. Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Donovan:

RE: Letter A-89-270

Thank you for your response to my request for a determination on a
potential mass mailing problem. My subsequent discussions with
Ms. Blanca Breeze of your office clarified sections of 18901.

I would appreciate a written confirmation of the telephone advice
provided to me on June 27, 1989 by Ms. Breeze regarding Sections
18901(e).

Sincerely,

James Gallagher
Assessor

the document, or (2) The document is sent at the request or

prohibitions of Section 89001 and Regulation 18901, you must

Our File No. A-89-270
Page 3

delete your name, title and reference to your office from the
card.

I trust we have adequately addressed your inquiry. Should
you have any further questions regarding this matter, do not
hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Counsel, Legal Division

KED/BMB:aa

Enclosure

[Sonoma County Seal]

James J. Gallagher
Sonoma County
Assessor

585 Fiscal Drive
Room 104F
Santa Rosa, CA 95403-2872
(707) 527-2541

May 2, 1989

Kathryn E. Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Donovan:

When I assumed office in January, 1987, I initiated a public information program in the Sonoma County Assessor's Office. One element of the program is the enclosed card which describes property tax exemptions. I made arrangements with the County Recorder to enclose a card with each recorded deed that is returned to a new property owner. Approximately 28,000 deeds were recorded in our county last year. I believe this mailing is essential to inform property owners of tax relief benefits administered by the Assessor's office.

It has recently come to my attention that this card may be considered a mass mailing. I would appreciate your opinion. I have suspended the mailings until I hear from you.

Sincerely,

[Signature]

James Gallagher
Assessor

JG/bb

cc: Richard Flores, Sonoma County Counsel
    Blanca Breece, Counsel FPPC

May 5, 1989


James Gallagher
Sonoma County Assessor
565 Fiscal Drive, Room 104F
Santa Rosa, CA  95403-2872

Re:  Leter No. 89-270

Dear Mr. Gallagher:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on May 4, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission.  If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact Blanca Breeze an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

James J. Gallagher
Sonoma County
Assessor

585 Fiscal Drive
Room 104F
Santa Rosa, CA 95403-2872
(707) 527-2541

June 28, 1989

Ms. Kathryn E. Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Donovan:

RE: Letter A-89-270

Thank you for your response to my request for a determination on a potential mass mailing problem. My subsequent discussions with Ms. Blanca Breeze of your office clarified sections of 18901.

I would appreciate a written confirmation of the telephone advice provided to me on June 27, 1989 by Ms. Breeze regarding Sections 18901(e).

Sincerely,

James Gallagher
Assessor

JG/bb

July 6, 1989


James Gallagher
Sonoma County Assessor
585 Fiscal Drive, Room 104F
Santa Rosa, CA  95403-2872

                                        Re:  Letter No. 89-393

Dear Mr. Gallagher:

     We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice
under the Political Reform Act on June 30, 1989. Your letter has
been assigned to Blanca Breeze for response. If you have any
questions, you may contact her directly at (916) 322-5901.

     If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. A confirming
response will be released after it has gone through our approval
process. If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you
shortly to advise you. In such cases, the normal analysis, review
and approval process will be followed.

     You should be aware that your letter and our response are
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        [illegible]

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

your office in a mass mailing violate the mass mailing prohibi-
tions of the Act?

CONCLUSION: Inclusion of your name and title and of the name of your of-
fice in a mass mailing violates the mass mailing prohibitions of 
the Act.
